d31c12ae21bcc097e65698b189841619e20957d8: {"teaching_note": "Predicting SalePrice means fitting a line (well, a many-dimensional version of one) that turns a house's measurable traits — its size, quality, age, and location — into a best-guess dollar figure, the same way simple linear regression draws the straight line through a scatter of points. Each feature you pick gets its own weight, and the model learns how many dollars a bump in that feature tends to add or subtract from the price.", "split_note": "You're choosing to set aside a random one-fifth of these 1,460 houses and hide their real prices while the model learns from the other four-fifths. Then you check the model's guesses against those held-out prices — an honesty test that shows whether it truly learned what drives price or just memorized the houses it already saw.", "features": finish that can raise perceived value."}, {"name": "OverallCond", "reason": "A 1-to-9 rating of the home's overall condition tells the model how well-maintained the house is."}]}
